architecture.summary: NeSyCat Torch interprets neurosymbolic semantics via differentiable tensor implementations of categorical logic. The system encodes first-order predicates and functions as neural networks, implementing truth-value aggregation through monadic semantics. Information flows from symbolic axioms written in monad-based do-notation through a parametric monad (distribution, log-tensor, or batch) that performs lazy pruning and marginalisation via monadic bind. Outputs are tensor-based implementations (HaskTorch, JAX, PyTorch backends) that support both symbolic reasoning and end-to-end differentiation.

biblio.title: NeSyCat Torch: A Differentiable Tensor Implementation of Categorical Semantics for Neurosymbolic Learning

claims.statement: NeSyCat subsumes classical, fuzzy, probabilistic and neural systems under a single inductive definition of truth, parametric in a strong monad and an aggregation structure on truth-values
The lazy log-tensor monad over the log-semiring enables numerically stable, differentiable training with lazy pruning of unneeded branches
On MNIST addition, HaskTorch, JAX, and PyTorch implementations outperform LTN and DeepProbLog in speed and accuracy
NeSyCat Torch achieves nearly the accuracy of DeepStochLog on MNIST addition
Unlike DeepStochLog, the framework applies uniformly to many first-order neurosymbolic approaches
The construction is parametric in the monad; instantiating it with the Giry monad extends the approach to continuous probability
